backend/main.mo: Empty Motoko actor serving as the ICP backend canister placeholder with no implemented methods.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping two features—generating a light-background logos image and integrating it into App.tsx—to their associated files, components, and test IDs.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document providing short descriptions of the three main frontend source files: App.tsx, index.css, and tailwind.config.js.
frontend/index.css: Base Tailwind CSS entry point defining OKLCH CSS custom properties for a neutral light/dark color theme used by shadcn-ui components.
frontend/index.html: HTML shell for the Ghuge Medicals React app, mounting the root div and loading the main.tsx module entry point.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Main React component for the Ghuge Medicals pharmaceutical distributor site, rendering sections for business hours, contact info, services, and a pharmaceutical partners logo image on a light background.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom React hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor using React Query, recreating it when the Internet Identity changes and invalidating dependent queries.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Custom hook and context provider managing Internet Identity authentication state, exposing login/logout functions and status flags via an AuthClient wrapper.
frontend/src/index.css: Global CSS with a medical-themed OKLCH color system featuring deep green primary and blue accent tokens for both light and dark modes, plus system font smoothing.
frontend/src/main.tsx: React app entry point that wraps the App component with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider before mounting to the DOM.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the default theme with OKLCH-based medical color tokens, a system font stack, custom border radii, accordion keyframes, and typography/container-queries plugins.
project_state.json: Tracks the state of the 'Ghuge Medicals' website project, describing requirements to display 5 partner logos using a combined light-background image in a compact section of App.tsx. Records existing features, architectural notes, and image editing requirements.
